---
title: "Capstone Project: DIA Proteomics Analysis"
author: "Course Team"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

# Overview

In this capstone project, each student team will analyze DIA (Data-Independent Acquisition) proteomics data. The objective is to perform protein identification using assigned tools and ensure proper data annotation, reproducibility, and standardized data sharing.

---

# Data Distribution

## DIA Data
- The dataset consists of **84 DIA files in `.mzML` format**
- Files will be shared via:
  - External hard drive **or**
  - https://filesender.funet.fi/

## Protein Database
- FASTA files (target + contaminants) are provided via the project GitHub repository

---

# Workflow

## Step 1: Data Access
- Download all `.mzML` files
- Clone or download the GitHub repository containing FASTA files

---

## Step 2: Protein Identification (Tool-Specific)

Each group must use their assigned software.

⚠️ **Important:**  
For all tools, the **preferred output level is precursor/PSM-level (or closest equivalent in DIA workflows)**, in addition to protein-level summaries.

---

### Group 1 – OpenMS
- Workflow:
  - OpenSwathWorkflow → PyProphet → Protein inference
- Input:
  - `.mzML`
- Recommended outputs:
  - Feature-level tables (precursor level)
  - Protein-level results
- Notes:
  - Use PyProphet for statistical scoring (1% FDR)

---

### Group 2 – DIA-NN
- Input:
  - `.mzML`
- Mode:
  - Library-free
- Recommended outputs:
  - `report.tsv` (**precursor-level, required**)
  - `pg_matrix.tsv` (protein-level)
- Key settings:
  - FDR: 1% (precursor and protein)
  - Enable cross-run normalization if needed

---

### Group 3 – Spectronaut
- Mode:
  - directDIA
- Input:
  - `.mzML`
- Recommended outputs:
  - Precursor-level report (**preferred**)
  - Protein report
- Notes:
  - Apply Q-value filtering (1%)
  - Export long-format tables when possible

---

### Group 4 – MaxDIA (MaxQuant)
- Workflow:
  - MaxDIA within MaxQuant
- Input:
  - `.mzML`
- Recommended outputs:
  - `evidence.txt` (**closest to PSM/precursor-level, required**)
  - `proteinGroups.txt`
- Notes:
  - Ensure correct FASTA parsing selection
  - FDR set to 1%

---

# Step 3: SDRF Annotation (Mandatory)

Each group must prepare an SDRF (Sample and Data Relationship Format) file describing the experimental design.

- Use a software-generated template if available  
- Otherwise, use:
  - **lessSDRF app**

---

# SDRF Template (DIA Example)

---

# SDRF Validation Checklist ✅

Before submission to github under your team folder, ensure:

## File Format
- [ ] File is **tab-separated (.tsv)**
- [ ] No formatting issues (no merged cells)
- [ ] Header row is present

## Required Columns
- [ ] `source name` (unique per sample)
- [ ] `assay name` (unique per run)
- [ ] `comment[data file]` matches `.mzML` file names exactly

## Metadata Completeness
- [ ] Organism specified (e.g., Homo sapiens)
- [ ] Biological condition included (treatment/disease)
- [ ] Replicates clearly defined

## Consistency Checks
- [ ] No duplicate assay names
- [ ] File names exactly match shared `.mzML` files
- [ ] Consistent naming conventions

## Experimental Design
- [ ] Control vs treatment clearly defined
- [ ] Time points annotated (6h and 24h)
- [ ] Drug combinations clearly labeled

## Common Mistakes to Avoid
- ❌ Using spaces instead of tabs  
- ❌ Mismatched file names (e.g., `.raw` vs `.mzML`)  
- ❌ Missing replicate information  
- ❌ Inconsistent sample naming  

---

# Data Submission (Mandatory)

## Identification Results
- Upload via:
  - https://filesender.funet.fi/
- Share with the teaching team

⚠️ Include:
- **Precursor/PSM-level output (required)**
- Protein-level summary (recommended)
- Please add your team number and software name for every file that you will share from your team:
  - TeamN_SOFTWARE_FILENAME
  - for example: Team4_MaxDIA_evidence.txt

---

## SDRF File
- Upload to:
  - Your group-specific folder in GitHub

---

# Optional Analysis

## Statistical Analysis
You may perform:
- Differential expression
- PCA / clustering
- Pathway or enrichment analysis

## Submission
- Upload results to:
  - Your **team folder only**
- Do NOT upload to the root directory

---

# Folder Structure

```text
project-root/
├── team-1_openms/
│   ├── Team1_OpenMS_SDRF.tsv
│   ├── Team1_OpenMS_results.tsv
│   ├── Team1_OpenMS_parameters.txt
│   └── ...
├── team-2_diann/
│   ├── Team2_DIA-NN_SDRF.tsv
│   ├── Team2_DIA-NN_report.tsv
│   ├── Team2_DIA-NN_pg_matrix.tsv
│   └── ...
```
---
